2605.23651.title: How Human-Like Are Large Language Models? A Register-Aware Linguistic Evaluation Framework
2605.23651.paper_summary: While factual correctness and task-performance have been in focus of Large Language Model (LLM) research for a long time, the fundamental question of how human-like generated texts are on a linguistic level has been underexplored. From a corpus-linguistic perspective, language production is inherently context-dependent, with distinct communicative contexts giving rise to differences in frequencies and co-occurrence patterns of linguistic features. A text failing to adhere to these patterns can be content-wise correct, but still be unfavorable to human readers. In this work, we propose a context-aware evaluation framework in which human-likeness is assessed using a two-sample problem between the linguistic feature distribution of a human reference corpus for a given register and a corresponding LLM-generated corpus. We implement this framework using the Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) and the 67 lexico-grammatical features introduced by Biber, which are commonly applied in corpus linguistics. In our experiments, we compare seven instruction-tuned, open-source models across five English-language datasets spanning distinct registers against a human baseline. While across all tested setups, LLMs deviate from the human baseline, which models are closest to human language depends on the register and is not dictated by model size.
2605.23651.paper_summary_zh: 雖然事實正確性和任務表現長期以來一直是大型語言模型（LLM）研究的焦點，但人類生成文本在語言層面上有多像人類的根本問題卻尚未受到充分探討。從語料庫語言學的角度來看，語言產出本質上依賴於上下文，不同的交際上下文會導致語言特徵的頻率和共現模式產生差異。一篇未能遵循這些模式的文本在內容上可能是正確的，但對人類讀者來說仍然不利。在本研究中，我們提出了一個上下文感知的評估框架，在該框架中，通過對比給定語域的人類參考語料庫的語言特徵分佈和相應的LLM生成語料庫，來評估人類相似性。我們使用最大均值差異（MMD）和Biber提出的67個詞彙語法特徵來實施這一框架，這些特徵通常應用於語料庫語言學。在我們的實驗中，我們比較了七個經過指令調整的開源模型在五個涵蓋不同語域的英語數據集上的表現，並與人類基準進行對比。雖然在所有測試設置中，LLM都偏離了人類基準，但哪些模型最接近人類語言取決於語域，並不僅由模型大小決定。
2605.23651.author: Björn Nieth et.al.
2605.23651.authors: Björn Nieth, Marianna Gracheva, Michaela Mahlberg, Bjoern Eskofier, Emmanuelle Salin
